qa_method.name: QA-ML v3 pilot: feature-only T1 + decision-tree distillation
qa_method.description: Pilot scope: 3-class T1 shortcut failure-mode prediction (correct / undercount_missed_sat / overclaim_false_sat) across M_train = {9, 10, 12, 15, 18, 20, 21, 24, 25, 30} and held-out M_test = {7, 8, 11, 30, 45, 75}. Two baselines: sklearn LogisticRegression on the 23-feature qa_full_v3 packet (b, e, d, a, C, F, G, phi_b, phi_e, psi_b, psi_e, gcd_b_m, gcd_e_m, is_singularity, m, m_div_3, m_mod_3, m_mod_5, fac_2/3/5/7/11), and a max_depth=12 DecisionTreeClassifier that doubles as the T3 symbolic-extraction candidate. The pilot omits the qa_gcn baseline (v2 carry-over) and the rule_miner baseline (RIPPER/BRL) to test feasibility before scaling.
qa_method.observer_projection: Integer (b, e) discrete states in {1,...,m}^2 -> 23-int feature packet computed by tools.qa_ml.qa_features_v3 in raw integer arithmetic -> sklearn classifier (float-side observer). Boundary crossed once on input, once on output (predicted T1 class label). No float feedback into the QA layer.

datasets.name: qa_v3_train_test_split
datasets.source: tools.qa_ml.qa_dataset_v3.build_v3_dataset; M_train = {9, 10, 12, 15, 18, 20, 21, 24, 25, 30}; M_test = {7, 8, 11, 30, 45, 75} (note m=30 appears in both since the pilot tests in-distribution generalization within the [277] regime); ground truth from qa_orbit_rules.orbit_family and qa_orbit_rules.orbit_family_divisor_shortcut

calibration_provenance.learned_on: qa_full_v3 features are deterministic integer transforms with no learned thresholds; only the downstream sklearn classifiers are fit

diffs: extends per-state feature packet from qa_full (9 features) to qa_full_v3 (23 features) by adding mod-5 phase, gcd-with-modulus, factorization, and modulus-level fields
switches task from binary cosmos-vs-satellite to 3-class shortcut failure-mode prediction (correct / undercount / overclaim)
introduces decision-tree distillation as a T3 symbolic-extraction candidate alongside the LogReg baseline
omits the qa_gcn baseline in the pilot — to be added in v3.1 if pilot validates rediscovery

source_mapping.primary_source: Verified to agree with the canonical orbit_family
source_mapping.mapping_rationale: The v3 pilot tests whether feature-only learning + decision-tree distillation can rediscover the divisor-shortcut failure modes certified in [277] and [278]. Ground truth comes from qa_orbit_rules.orbit_family (canonical, period-based, fixed at commit e7b2af0) and orbit_family_divisor_shortcut (the algebraic helper whose failure surface [277] and [278] map). The pilot is a feasibility check for the broader v3 plan in docs/specs/QA_ML_V3_STRUCTURE_DISCOVERY_PLAN.md; success criterion is rediscover_277 and rediscover_278 each >= 0.95 on the held-out test moduli.

ablation.destroyed_structure: Two predictors with identical inputs and labels but different model class (LogisticRegression vs DecisionTreeClassifier with max_depth=12) — the tree's depth budget is the structural lever. A LogReg-as-strong-as-tree result indicates the failure modes are linearly separable in the v3 feature space; a tree-much-stronger-than-LogReg result indicates they require non-linear decisions and motivates the qa_gcn extension.
ablation.expected_direction: Tree macro_f1 > LogReg macro_f1; tree rediscovery scores (>=0.95 target) on [277] and [278] regimes. If tree falls short, the v3 plan's success criterion (rule rediscovery via distillation) is not met by this pilot and v3.1 must escalate to qa_gcn + rule mining or symbolic regression.
